MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-01-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER DENYING REGENTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE KAHN DISCLOSES THAT THE GENERAL COUNSEL OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA IS A CLOSE FRIEND OF HIS, BUT NEITHER THAT FRIENDSHIP NOR ANY OTHER MATTER CAUSES HIM TO BE OTHER THAN FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN DECIDING THIS CASE. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON THE SOLE REMAINING CAUSE OF ACTION FOR RETALIATION IN VIOLATION OF THE CALIFORNIA FAMILY RIGHTS ACT IS DENIED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-01-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Daniel Feder, The Law Offices of Daniel Feder, Tel. No. 1-415-391-9476
appearing for the plaintiff;
Atty. Ana L. Padgett of Ogletree/Deakins, Tel. No. 1-415-369-3547 appearing
for the Defendant.

RULING - Judge Kahn discloses that the General Counsel of the University of California is a close friend of his, but neither that friendship nor any other matter causes him to be other than fair and impartial in deciding this case. The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant The Regents of the University of California's motion for summary judgment on the sole remaining cause of action for retaliation in violation of the California Family Rights Act is denied. The Regents satisfied its summary judgment burden by showing that it had a legitimate reason for terminating Ms. Young's employment. The Regents (through Ms. Figert, Ms. Lopez, and others) concluded that Ms. Young violated the Regents' workplace violence policy by threatening Ms. Lopez on November 4, 2016. Plaintiff Cheryl Young submitted substantial responsive evidence that the Regents' proffered reason was pretextual. Ms. Lopez was one of the people who made the decision to terminate Ms. Young's employment. Ms. Young denies that she ever made any threats against Ms. Lopez. A reasonable trier of fact could conclude that the Regents' reason for the termination of Ms. Young's employment was false and that the true reason was Ms. Young took CFRA leave. The evidence submitted by Ms. Young shows that the Regents wanted to terminate her employment for "job abandonment" or for taking family leave even though Ms. Young submitted the leave paperwork in a timely fashion and the leave was ultimately approved. Evidentiary Objections were ruled on the record. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
